examples.title: Basic sigaction Setup for SIGINT
examples.description: Demonstrates how to set up a signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C) using sigaction()
examples.explanation: This program sets up a signal handler for SIGINT using sigaction(). The sa.sa_handler points to the custom handler, sa.sa_mask is empty (no blocking), and sa_flags is 0 for default behavior. sigaction() returns -1 on error which is handled.

examples.title: Extended Signal Handler with SA_SIGINFO
examples.description: Shows how to use SA_SIGINFO flag to get sender process information
examples.explanation: When SA_SIGINFO flag is set, sa_sigaction is used instead of sa_handler. The handler receives three parameters: signal number, siginfo_t pointer (containing sender info), and context. This allows the program to know which process sent the signal.

examples.title: Signal Blocking During Handler Execution
examples.description: Demonstrates using sa_mask to block signals while handler is running
examples.explanation: This demonstrates signal blocking during handler execution. The sa_mask in sigaction can specify signals to block automatically. Additionally, sigprocmask() can be used inside the handler for more granular control over which signals are blocked during critical sections.

examples.title: Signal Mask Manipulation with sigprocmask
examples.description: Shows how to block and unblock signals using sigprocmask()
examples.explanation: sigprocmask() manipulates the process signal mask. SIG_BLOCK adds signals to block, SIG_UNBLOCK removes them, and SIG_SETMASK replaces the mask. When a blocked signal is raised, it's held until unblocked, then the handler executes.
